title: Lemuria
summary: The other lost paradise — and the Lemurian Kingdom that still holds a diplomatic seat.
markdown:
**Lemuria** stands beside [Atlantis](/lore/atlantis/) in the archive's memory of the world
before the Fall. The April 23, 2020 session invokes it as the benchmark of what restoration
means: the planet, Unimetrix 1 promises, *"will be restored to pristine conditions such as they
were before the fall of Lemuria and Atlantis"* — the two names bracketing the golden age
together, the ice-shielded garden world of the
[giant light-beings](/lore/rainbow-light-body/).

But Lemuria is not only a memory. In the same session, listing the bodies a future ambassador
would stand between, the archive names — alongside the
[Galactic Federation](/lore/interstellar-alliance/) and "the Agarthean Kingdom" — **"the
Lemurian Kingdom"**: a still-extant polity holding a seat at the table. Like
[Agartha](/lore/agartha/), Lemuria survives in the archive's present as a hidden kingdom
rather than a ruin — continuous with its own deep past, waiting on the surface world to
rejoin the conversation.

Pre-cataclysm paradise and living kingdom at once, Lemuria completes the archive's map of the
elder Earth: what was lost above the waves, and what endured beyond them.
